steps.title: Identify the slope of line l
steps.content: Line $k$ has slope $m$. Since perpendicular lines have slopes that multiply to $-1$, line $l$ has slope $-\frac{1}{m}$.

steps.title: Write the equation of line l
steps.content:
Line $l$ passes through $(2, 8)$ with slope $-\frac{1}{m}$. Using point-slope form:
$$y - 8 = -\frac{1}{m}(x - 2)$$

steps.title: Evaluate the value at x = 3
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 3$:
$$y - 8 = -\frac{1}{m}(3 - 2) = -\frac{1}{m}$$
$$y = 8 - \frac{1}{m}$$
The point on line $l$ is $(3, 8 - \frac{1}{m})$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
